name: Cassoulet De Poissons
recipeIngredient: 3/4 lb Dried white beans (such as Great northern)
3/4 lb Tomatoes, peeled, seeded and chopped
2 Trout (about 1 lb each) or 30 oz whiting (6 @ 5oz each)
recipeInstructions:
1. Soak the beans overnight in cold water to cover. Drain and rinse.
Transfer to a large saucepan and cover with 4 to 5 inches of fresh cold water.
Peel the whole onion and stud with the clove; add to the pan along with the bouquet garni.
Bring to a boil and skim the froth that rises to the surface.
Reduce heat and simmer for 1 1/2 hours. Season to taste with salt.
Add the carrots and continue simmering until the carrots and beans are tender, about 30 minutes.
Drain; discard the bouquet garni and onion.
2. Heat 3 tablespoons oil and 2 tablespoons butter in a frying pan over low heat.
Add the chopped onions and cook slowly, stirring occasionally, until tender but not colored.
Add garlic and cook one minute.
Add tomatoes, season with salt and pepper, and simmer for 15 minutes.
3. Clean the trout (or whiting) and cut into 1 inch slices. Cut the monkfish fillets into 1 inch thick slices.
Cut the scallops in half cross-wise.
Heat remaining 3 tablespoons oil and 2 tablespoons butter in a large frying pan over high heat.
Add the trout (or whiting) slices and sauté until golden brown on both sides; set aside.
Brown the monkfish and sliced scallops in the same way; set aside.
4. Preheat oven to 450°F.
5. Combine tomato mixture and drained beans in a saucepan and simmer for 5 minutes.
Taste and adjust seasonings.
Spread half of the mixture over the bottom of a 10x15 inch gratin dish.
Arrange fish and scallops on top and cover with remaining bean mixture.
Sprinkle with bread crumbs and dot with remaining 2 tablespoons butter.
Bake until bread crumbs are golden brown, 10-15 minutes.
